name: throttle
description: The standalone lodash.throttle package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want predictable throttling behavior without adding the package to your app dependency graph.
tagline: Limit repeated calls to a steady rate.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.throttle file in both raw and normalized forms. The goal is to vendor the known behavior into your repo, not to rewrite throttling from scratch.
nativeAlternative: setTimeout or requestAnimationFrame can approximate throttling, but they do not automatically match lodash's contract around leading or trailing calls, cancel, flush, and retained arguments.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
